title: thelypteris hispiduliformis
intro: Thelypteris hispiduliformis, also known as the hairy-leaved maiden fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Thelypteris hispiduliformis include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
